response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned staff-level individual contributor with 25+ years of hands-on backend and full-stack engineering experience, but his profile does not align with the executive leadership requirements of this CTO role. There is no evidence of people management, team scaling, board-level communication, or organizational ownership at any point in his career — he has remained an IC throughout. His agentic-AI exposure is recent and described in general terms (MCP tooling, agentic workflow support), with no demonstrated production multi-agent systems operating at financial scale or in regulated environments. Fintech depth is limited to two short-tenure roles with no credit infrastructure, payment-flow, or compliance-engineering background. The role requires an executive who has built and scaled engineering organizations and shipped AI systems with real financial consequences — neither is evidenced here.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and high-throughput backend pedigree (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency microservices in DLang)
Recent hands-on engagement with agentic AI tooling and MCP frameworks at re:cinq signals genuine domain interest and early technical exposure
Exceptionally broad technical range across stacks, languages, and paradigms (event-driven, embedded, mobile, GIS, web) demonstrating architectural adaptability
concerns: Zero people-management or team-scaling history — entire 25-year career is as an individual contributor, a fundamental disqualifier for a CTO expected to grow a 30+ person org
Agentic AI experience is vaguely scoped and recent with no evidence of production multi-agent systems carrying financial or regulatory consequences
No meaningful fintech-infrastructure background — credit systems, working-capital flows, real-time risk modeling, and multi-jurisdiction compliance are entirely absent
